title: Bridge Essay: Missing images: autobiographical memory in Aphantasia and blindness.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Missing, images:, autobiographical, memory, in.

**Missing images: autobiographical memory in Aphantasia and blindness.** — Front Cognit. PMID: 42339253. DOI: 10.3389/fcogn.2025.1644533. Mental visual imagery, especially the ability to construct naturalistic scenes seems central to vivid episodic autobiographical memory (AM). This mini review will first highlight the neural anatomy of different aspects of mental imagery, focusing on the roles of the hippocampus, ventromedial prefrontal cortex and posterior neocortex and the consequences of damage to these regions to AM.
